steps.title: Identify the standard circle equation and extract values
steps.content: A circle with center $(h, k)$ and radius $r$ follows a standard form. From the problem: center is $(19, 16)$ (so $h = 19$, $k = 16$) and radius is $8k$ (so $r = 8k$).

steps.title: Substitute and square the radius
steps.content: Substitute into the formula: $(x-19)^2 + (y-16)^2 = (8k)^2$. Calculate $(8k)^2 = 64k^2$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
